facts.text: Mineola’s name comes from the Algonquin word “Miniolagamika,” meaning “pleasant village,” and was first used when the area was called Hempstead Branch.
NYU Langone Hospital – Long Island, founded in 1896 as Nassau Hospital, was Long Island’s first voluntary hospital and remains a nationally recognized medical center.
On May 20, 1927, Charles Lindbergh launched his historic trans‑Atlantic flight from the nearby Long Island airfield, a moment that put Mineola on the world map.
Mineola’s vibrant Portuguese community, visible in local cuisine and businesses, has shaped the village’s cultural identity for decades.
The village celebrated its centennial in 2006 with parades, concerts, and a community‑wide tribute that highlighted Mineola’s rich history.
Mineola’s first theater, Allen’s Hall, drew early moviegoers to the village, marking the beginning of its long tradition of entertainment.
